Residuals in Simple Linear Regression

R Tutorial Notes

This file builds two tiny examples from openintro::births14:

  1. A 5-row example where residual quartiles from summary(lm(...)) land exactly on observed points.
  2. A 6-row example where quartiles are interpolated.

The workflow is:

  • filter to preemies,
  • convert birth weight from lbs to kg,
  • slice_tail() to make the dataset intentionally small,
  • fit birth_wt_kg ~ weeks,
  • hand-compute residuals as y - ŷ,
  • compare the residual summary (Min, 1Q, Median, 3Q, Max),
  • connect this to residual standard error.
preemies <- openintro::births14 %>%
  filter(premie == "premie") %>%
  mutate(
    birth_wt_kg = weight * 0.45359237,
    obs_id = row_number()
  )

exact_data <- preemies %>% tail(5)
interp_data <- preemies %>% tail(6)
exact_fit <- lm(birth_wt_kg ~ weeks, data = exact_data)
exact_data <- exact_data %>%
  mutate(
    yhat = predict(exact_fit, newdata = exact_data),
    residual = birth_wt_kg - yhat,
    abs_residual = abs(residual),
    squared_residual = residual^2
  )

exact_summary <- summary(exact_fit)$coefficients
exact_resid_summary <- summary(exact_data$residual)
exact_quartiles <- quantile(exact_data$residual, probs = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), type = 7)

exact_resid_sorted <- exact_data %>% arrange(residual) %>%
  mutate(quartile_role = c("min", "Q1", "Median", "Q3", "max"))

cat("EXACT 5-ROW EXAMPLE (tail of preemies)\n")
## EXACT 5-ROW EXAMPLE (tail of preemies)
cat("Formula: y = intercept + slope * weeks\n")
## Formula: y = intercept + slope * weeks
cat(sprintf("y = %.6f + %.6f*weeks\n", exact_fit$coefficients[[1]], exact_fit$coefficients[[2]]))
## y = -14.315051 + 0.489556*weeks
cat(sprintf("n = %d\n\n", nrow(exact_data)))
## n = 5
cat("obs_id,weeks,weight_lb,weight_kg,y_hat,actual_minus_fitted\n")
## obs_id,weeks,weight_lb,weight_kg,y_hat,actual_minus_fitted
for (i in seq_len(nrow(exact_data))) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "%d,%d,%.2f,%.4f,%.4f,%.4f\n",
    exact_data$obs_id[i],
    exact_data$weeks[i],
    exact_data$weight[i],
    exact_data$birth_wt_kg[i],
    exact_data$yhat[i],
    exact_data$residual[i]
  ))
}
## 120,36,8.44,3.8283,3.3090,0.5194
## 121,35,5.58,2.5310,2.8194,-0.2884
## 122,36,5.82,2.6399,3.3090,-0.6690
## 123,32,2.33,1.0569,1.3507,-0.2939
## 124,34,6.75,3.0617,2.3298,0.7319
cat("\nresidual summary from R (exact 5-point fit)\n")
## 
## residual summary from R (exact 5-point fit)
cat(capture.output(exact_resid_summary), sep = "\n")
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
## -0.6690 -0.2939 -0.2884  0.0000  0.5194  0.7319
cat("\nquantiles extracted directly from quantile(..., type = 7)\n")
## 
## quantiles extracted directly from quantile(..., type = 7)
cat(sprintf("Min: %0.4f\n", exact_quartiles[[1]]))
## Min: -0.6690
cat(sprintf("1Q: %0.4f\n", exact_quartiles[[2]]))
## 1Q: -0.2939
cat(sprintf("Median: %0.4f\n", exact_quartiles[[3]]))
## Median: -0.2884
cat(sprintf("3Q: %0.4f\n", exact_quartiles[[4]]))
## 3Q: 0.5194
cat(sprintf("Max: %0.4f\n\n", exact_quartiles[[5]]))
## Max: 0.7319
kable(
  exact_data %>%
    select(obs_id, weeks, birth_wt_kg, yhat, residual) %>%
    mutate(
      hand_math = sprintf("%.4f - %.4f = %.4f", birth_wt_kg, yhat, residual)
    ),
  caption = "Exact 5-row example: exact hand computation y - ŷ = residual",
  digits = 4
)
Exact 5-row example: exact hand computation y - <U+0177> = residual
obs_id weeks birth_wt_kg yhat residual hand_math
120 36 3.8283 3.3090 0.5194 3.8283 - 3.3090 = 0.5194
121 35 2.5310 2.8194 -0.2884 2.5310 - 2.8194 = -0.2884
122 36 2.6399 3.3090 -0.6690 2.6399 - 3.3090 = -0.6690
123 32 1.0569 1.3507 -0.2939 1.0569 - 1.3507 = -0.2939
124 34 3.0617 2.3298 0.7319 3.0617 - 2.3298 = 0.7319 
ggplot(exact_data, aes(weeks, birth_wt_kg)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1, color = "black") +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = weeks, yend = yhat), alpha = 0.8, linewidth = 1, color = "tomato") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(residual, 3), y = yhat),
    vjust = -1,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Exact 5-row example: observed points and residual segments",
    x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
    y = "Birth weight (kg)",
    subtitle = "Vertical segments are residuals (observed - fitted)"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(exact_data, aes(weeks, residual)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1, color = "gray40") +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = weeks, y = 0, yend = residual), linewidth = 1, color = "tomato") +
  geom_point(aes(color = residual > 0), size = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(residual, 3)), vjust = -0.9, size = 3.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "steelblue", "FALSE" = "darkred"), guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Residuals with zero line (5-row case)",
    x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
    y = "Residual (kg)",
    subtitle = "Negative = below line, Positive = above line"
  )

In this 5-point example, the residual quartiles are exactly equal to observed residual points because with type-7 quantiles and n = 5, the positions become:

  • .25*(n-1)+1 = 2,
  • .50*(n-1)+1 = 3,
  • .75*(n-1)+1 = 4.

So min, 1Q, median, 3Q, and max align to the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th residuals in sorted order.

Second example: quartile values are interpolated

interp_fit <- lm(birth_wt_kg ~ weeks, data = interp_data)
interp_data <- interp_data %>%
  mutate(
    yhat = predict(interp_fit, newdata = interp_data),
    residual = birth_wt_kg - yhat,
    abs_residual = abs(residual),
    squared_residual = residual^2
  )

interp_summary <- summary(interp_data$residual)
interp_quartiles <- quantile(interp_data$residual, probs = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1), type = 7)
interp_sorted <- interp_data %>% arrange(residual)
interp_n <- nrow(interp_data)
interp_probs <- c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1)
interp_h <- (interp_n - 1) * interp_probs + 1

cat("INTERPOLATED 6-ROW EXAMPLE (tail of preemies)\n")
## INTERPOLATED 6-ROW EXAMPLE (tail of preemies)
cat(sprintf("n = %d\n", interp_n))
## n = 6
cat(sprintf("y = %.6f + %.6f*weeks\n", interp_fit$coefficients[[1]], interp_fit$coefficients[[2]]))
## y = -13.102016 + 0.455860*weeks
cat("\nobs_id,weeks,weight_lb,weight_kg,y_hat,actual_minus_fitted\n")
## 
## obs_id,weeks,weight_lb,weight_kg,y_hat,actual_minus_fitted
for (i in seq_len(nrow(interp_data))) {
  cat(sprintf(
    "%d,%d,%.2f,%.4f,%.4f,%.4f\n",
    interp_data$obs_id[i],
    interp_data$weeks[i],
    interp_data$weight[i],
    interp_data$birth_wt_kg[i],
    interp_data$yhat[i],
    interp_data$residual[i]
  ))
}
## 119,33,4.80,2.1772,1.9414,0.2359
## 120,36,8.44,3.8283,3.3090,0.5194
## 121,35,5.58,2.5310,2.8531,-0.3221
## 122,36,5.82,2.6399,3.3090,-0.6690
## 123,32,2.33,1.0569,1.4855,-0.4286
## 124,34,6.75,3.0617,2.3972,0.6645
cat("\nresidual summary from R (6-point fit)\n")
## 
## residual summary from R (6-point fit)
cat(capture.output(interp_summary), sep = "\n")
##     Min.  1st Qu.   Median     Mean  3rd Qu.     Max. 
## -0.66905 -0.40200 -0.04309  0.00000  0.44849  0.66451
cat("\nquantiles from quantile(..., type = 7)\n")
## 
## quantiles from quantile(..., type = 7)
cat(sprintf("Min: %0.4f\n", interp_quartiles[[1]]))
## Min: -0.6690
cat(sprintf("1Q: %0.4f\n", interp_quartiles[[2]]))
## 1Q: -0.4020
cat(sprintf("Median: %0.4f\n", interp_quartiles[[3]]))
## Median: -0.0431
cat(sprintf("3Q: %0.4f\n", interp_quartiles[[4]]))
## 3Q: 0.4485
cat(sprintf("Max: %0.4f\n\n", interp_quartiles[[5]]))
## Max: 0.6645
interp_q_table <- tibble(
  stat = c("Min", "Q1", "Median", "Q3", "Max"),
  prob = interp_probs,
  h = interp_h,
  lower_idx = floor(interp_h),
  upper_idx = ceiling(interp_h),
  lower_resid = interp_sorted$residual[floor(interp_h)],
  upper_resid = interp_sorted$residual[ceiling(interp_h)],
  w = interp_h - floor(interp_h),
  quantile_value = unname(interp_quartiles)
) %>%
  mutate(
    interpolated = (1 - w) * lower_resid + w * upper_resid,
    exact_match = abs(quantile_value - interpolated) < 1e-10,
    formula_note = ifelse(
      floor(h) == ceiling(h),
      "exact point",
      sprintf("((1-%.2f)*x[%d]) + (%.2f*x[%d])", w, lower_idx, w, upper_idx)
    )
  )

cat("What the quartile calculator is doing for the 6-point case:\n")
## What the quartile calculator is doing for the 6-point case:
print(interp_q_table)
## # A tibble: 5 x 12
##   stat    prob     h lower_idx upper_idx lower_resid upper_resid     w
##   <chr>  <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl>
## 1 Min     0     1            1         1      -0.669      -0.669  0   
## 2 Q1      0.25  2.25         2         3      -0.429      -0.322  0.25
## 3 Median  0.5   3.5          3         4      -0.322       0.236  0.5 
## 4 Q3      0.75  4.75         4         5       0.236       0.519  0.75
## 5 Max     1     6            6         6       0.665       0.665  0   
## # i 4 more variables: quantile_value <dbl>, interpolated <dbl>,
## #   exact_match <lgl>, formula_note <chr>
cat("\nInterpreting this table:\n")
## 
## Interpreting this table:
cat("- In this case, Q1 and Q3 are usually fractional positions (e.g., 2.75 and 4.75).\n")
## - In this case, Q1 and Q3 are usually fractional positions (e.g., 2.75 and 4.75).
cat("- That means R is taking weighted blends of neighboring residuals; these are not\n")
## - That means R is taking weighted blends of neighboring residuals; these are not
cat("- necessarily equal to any observed residual.\n\n")
## - necessarily equal to any observed residual.
kable(interp_q_table, digits = 4, caption = "How type-7 interpolation creates quartiles when n = 6")
How type-7 interpolation creates quartiles when n = 6
stat prob h lower_idx upper_idx lower_resid upper_resid w quantile_value interpolated exact_match formula_note
Min 0.00 1.00 1 1 -0.6690 -0.6690 0.00 -0.6690 -0.6690 TRUE exact point
Q1 0.25 2.25 2 3 -0.4286 -0.3221 0.25 -0.4020 -0.4020 TRUE ((1-0.25)x[2]) + (0.25x[3])
Median 0.50 3.50 3 4 -0.3221 0.2359 0.50 -0.0431 -0.0431 TRUE ((1-0.50)x[3]) + (0.50x[4])
Q3 0.75 4.75 4 5 0.2359 0.5194 0.75 0.4485 0.4485 TRUE ((1-0.75)x[4]) + (0.75x[5])
Max 1.00 6.00 6 6 0.6645 0.6645 0.00 0.6645 0.6645 TRUE exact point 
ggplot(interp_data, aes(weeks, birth_wt_kg)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1, color = "black") +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = weeks, yend = yhat), linewidth = 1, color = "tomato") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = round(residual, 3), y = yhat),
    vjust = -1,
    size = 3.5
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Interpolation case: residual segments are still computed the same way",
    x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
    y = "Birth weight (kg)",
    subtitle = "But summary quantiles are not all from one observed residual"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot(interp_data, aes(weeks, residual)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1, color = "gray40") +
  geom_segment(aes(xend = weeks, y = 0, yend = residual), linewidth = 1, color = "tomato") +
  geom_point(aes(color = residual > 0), size = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(residual, 3)), vjust = -0.9, size = 3.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "steelblue", "FALSE" = "darkred"), guide = "none") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Residuals with zero line (6-row case)",
    x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
    y = "Residual (kg)",
    subtitle = "Quartile lines may land between points and are interpolated"
  )

Residual standard error (RSE)

Residual standard error is the “typical size” of residuals, but on the original units (kg).

  • We cannot average residuals directly because positive and negative errors cancel and sum to ~0.
  • We cannot just average absolute residuals if you want a statistic that works nicely in algebra.
  • So we square residuals, sum those squares (RSS), divide by n - p, then square-root.
  • p = 2 here because a simple line has 2 estimated coefficients (intercept, slope).
rse_data <- exact_data
rss <- sum(rse_data$residual^2)
n <- nrow(rse_data)
p <- 2
rse <- sqrt(rss / (n - p))

cat("RSE (exact 5-row example)\n")
## RSE (exact 5-row example)
cat(sprintf("RSS = %.6f\n", rss))
## RSS = 1.422552
cat(sprintf("n = %d\n", n))
## n = 5
cat(sprintf("p = %d\n", p))
## p = 2
cat(sprintf("RSE = sqrt(RSS / (n - p)) = sqrt(%.6f / %d) = %.6f\n", rss, n - p, rse))
## RSE = sqrt(RSS / (n - p)) = sqrt(1.422552 / 3) = 0.688610
cat("\nQuick comparison with lm output:\n")
## 
## Quick comparison with lm output:
cat(sprintf("sigma = %0.6f (this should match)\n", summary(exact_fit)$sigma))
## sigma = 0.688610 (this should match)
kable(
  rse_data %>%
    summarise(
      RSS = sum(residual^2),
      n = n(),
      `n - p` = n() - 2,
      RSE = sqrt(sum(residual^2) / (n() - 2))
    ),
  digits = 6,
  caption = "Residual standard error formula for the exact 5-row model"
)
Residual standard error formula for the exact 5-row model
RSS n n - p RSE
1.422552 5 3 0.68861 
cat("What to take into the slides:\n")
## What to take into the slides:
cat("- RSE is the standard deviation-like scale for model residuals in original units.\n")
## - RSE is the standard deviation-like scale for model residuals in original units.
cat("- Min/1Q/Median/3Q/Max in model output are summary quantities of the sorted residuals.\n")
## - Min/1Q/Median/3Q/Max in model output are summary quantities of the sorted residuals.
cat("- For small n (here n=5), some quartiles can coincide exactly with observed residuals.\n")
## - For small n (here n=5), some quartiles can coincide exactly with observed residuals.
cat("- For other n values, RSE computation stays the same, but quartiles can be interpolated.\n")
## - For other n values, RSE computation stays the same, but quartiles can be interpolated.
